id.description: Primary key for each chart of accounts record.
id.annotation_note: Primary key identifier. No financial meaning.

businessID.description: Unique identifier for the business associated with this chart of accounts record.
businessID.annotation_note: Foreign key identifier. No financial meaning.

Account_name.description: The name of the account as it appears in the chart of accounts.
Account_name.annotation_note: Text label identifying an account. Not a measure. Used in D1 checks to confirm SQL is operating on an account-scoped entity.

Account_type.description: The IFRS element category of the account (e.g. asset, liability, equity, income, expense).
Account_type.annotation_note: Stores the account type label as text. This is the classifier column for account-level financial scope. Machine-readable value_concepts map actual BookSQL Account_type values, such as expenses, income, other expense, and accounts receivable (a/r), to broader financial concepts for schema-grounded SQL semantic mapping and decompilation. This is schema-level metadata and does not encode question-specific gold SQL logic.

id.description: Primary key for each customer record.
id.annotation_note: Primary key identifier. No financial meaning.

businessID.description: Identifier linking the customer to a business.
businessID.annotation_note: Foreign key identifier. No financial meaning.

customer_name.description: Short name or alias of the customer.
customer_name.annotation_note: Text identifier. Used for entity filtering. Not a measure.

customer_full_name.description: Full legal name of the customer.

Billing_address.description: Billing street address of the customer.

Billing_ZIP_code.description: Billing ZIP code of the customer.
Billing_ZIP_code.annotation_note: Postal code stored as integer. Not a financial measure.

Shipping_address.description: Shipping street address of the customer.

Shipping_ZIP_code.description: Shipping ZIP code of the customer.
Shipping_ZIP_code.annotation_note: Postal code stored as integer. Not a financial measure.

Balance.description: Outstanding accounts receivable balance owed by this customer.
Balance.annotation_note: AR balance per customer. Assets are debit-normal. Stock measure — represents an amount owed at a point in time, not accumulated over a period. A question asking for customer balance should use this column, not transaction Amount or Credit. Using SUM(Credit) instead of this column is a D2 violation.

id.description: Primary key for each employee record.

businessID.description: Identifier linking the employee to a business.

Employee_name.annotation_note: Text identifier. Not a financial measure.

Hire_date.description: The date the employee was hired.
Hire_date.annotation_note: Date field. Not a financial measure.

Billing_rate.description: The hourly or unit billing rate associated with this employee.
Billing_rate.annotation_note: Rate applied per unit of work. Treated as a cost/expense-side value. Debit-normal as expenses increase on the debit side. Not the same as total billed amount — it is the rate per unit, not a cumulative measure.

Deleted.description: Flag indicating whether the employee record has been soft-deleted.
Deleted.annotation_note: Boolean-style flag stored as text. No financial meaning.

id.description: Primary key for each transaction line record.

businessID.description: Identifier linking the transaction to a business.

Transaction_ID.description: Identifier grouping transaction lines that belong to the same transaction.
Transaction_ID.annotation_note: Groups rows into a single transaction. Not a measure.

Transaction_DATE.description: The date on which the transaction occurred.
Transaction_DATE.annotation_note: Primary date field used for period filtering. Critical for D3 temporal scope checks. A question asking for monthly revenue should filter this column to the correct month range.

Transaction_TYPE.description: The type of financial transaction (e.g. Invoice, Expense, Bill, Payment).
Transaction_TYPE.annotation_note: Categorical classifier for transaction kind. Machine-readable value_concepts map transaction type values to broader transaction concepts for schema-grounded SQL semantic mapping and decompilation. This is schema-level metadata and does not encode question-specific gold SQL logic.

Amount.description: Gross transaction amount without directional sign convention.
Amount.annotation_note: AMBIGUOUS — sign_convention is none because Amount does not distinguish credit-side from debit-side values. It is the most common source of D2 violations: a question asking for invoice value (credit-normal) should use Credit, not Amount. Using Amount where Credit is required is a financial measure error. Use sign_convention=ambiguous because this column does not encode debit/credit direction.

CreatedDATE.description: The system date when the transaction record was created.
CreatedDATE.annotation_note: System creation timestamp, not the transaction date. Should not be used for financial period filtering. Using this instead of Transaction_DATE is a D3 temporal scope error.

CreatedUSER.description: The user who created the transaction record.

Account.description: The account name this transaction line is posted to.
Account.annotation_note: Text reference to the account name. Used in D1 checks — questions about account-level aggregation should filter on this column, not on Product_Service. Confusing the two is a D1 entity_scope violation.

AR_paid.description: Flag indicating whether the accounts receivable for this transaction has been paid.
AR_paid.annotation_note: Boolean-style status flag. Useful for filtering outstanding vs paid AR. Not a monetary measure.

AP_paid.description: Flag indicating whether the accounts payable for this transaction has been paid.
AP_paid.annotation_note: Boolean-style status flag. Useful for filtering outstanding vs paid AP. Not a monetary measure.

Due_DATE.description: The payment due date for this transaction.
Due_DATE.annotation_note: Date field for payment due dates. Distinct from Transaction_DATE. Should not be used as a proxy for transaction period.

Open_balance.description: The remaining unpaid balance on this transaction at the time of recording.
Open_balance.annotation_note: Outstanding balance per transaction line. Stock measure — represents what is still owed at a point in time, not a period flow. account_type is intentionally none — this column appears on both AR transactions (asset, debit-normal) and AP transactions (liability, credit-normal) depending on context. The financial class of this balance must be inferred from Transaction_TYPE, Customers, or Vendor columns, not from this column alone. D2 violation: using SUM(Amount) or SUM(Credit) instead of this column for questions about outstanding balances. Use sign_convention=context_dependent because the balance meaning depends on AR/AP or transaction context.

Customers.description: The customer name associated with this transaction.
Customers.annotation_note: Text reference to customer. Used for customer-level filtering.

Vendor.description: The vendor name associated with this transaction.
Vendor.annotation_note: Text reference to vendor. Used for vendor-level filtering.

Product_Service.description: The product or service line item associated with this transaction.
Product_Service.annotation_note: Text reference to a product or service. entity_scope is product_service, NOT account. A question about account categories (e.g. income, expense) should NOT filter on this column. Filtering on Product_Service when the question asks about an account category is a D1 entity_scope violation — the most common D1 error in BookSQL.

Quantity.description: The number of units for the product or service on this transaction line.
Quantity.annotation_note: Integer count of units. Not a monetary value. Should not be summed in place of monetary amounts.

Rate.description: The unit price or rate applied to the product or service on this transaction line.
Rate.annotation_note: Per-unit price. Not the total line value. Total = Quantity x Rate. Should not be summed directly to get revenue — that ignores quantity.

Credit.description: The credit-side monetary value of this transaction line.
Credit.annotation_note: Credit-side monetary value. sign_convention: credit_normal is the key attribute for D2 checks. account_type is intentionally none — credit entries can relate to income, liability, or equity depending on the account being posted to. The account classification comes from the Account column and chart_of_accounts.Account_type, not from this column. D2 violation: using Amount (sign_convention: none) instead of Credit when the question requires a credit-normal measure such as invoice value or revenue.

Debit.description: The debit-side monetary value of this transaction line.
Debit.annotation_note: Debit-side monetary value. sign_convention: debit_normal is the key attribute for D2 checks. account_type is intentionally none — debit entries can increase assets or expenses depending on the account being posted to. The account classification comes from the Account column and chart_of_accounts.Account_type, not from this column. D2 violation: using Amount or Credit instead of Debit when the question requires a debit-normal measure such as total expenses or costs paid.

payment_method.description: The payment method used for this transaction (e.g. cash, cheque, credit card).
payment_method.annotation_note: Categorical label. Used for filtering by payment type. Not a measure.

Misc.description: Miscellaneous notes or metadata associated with the transaction.

id.description: Primary key for each payment method record.

businessID.description: Identifier linking the payment method to a business.

Payment_method.description: Name or label of the payment method (e.g. Cash, Bank Transfer).
Payment_method.annotation_note: Text label. No financial meaning beyond filtering.

Credit_card.description: Flag or name indicating whether this payment method is a credit card.
Credit_card.annotation_note: Text flag — NOT a financial credit column. Do not confuse with master_txn_table.Credit. sign_convention is none.

id.description: Primary key for each product or service record.

businessID.description: Identifier linking the product to a business.

Product_Service.description: Name of the product or service.
Product_Service.annotation_note: Product or service name. entity_scope is product_service. This is frequently confused with account-level filtering in D1 violations.

Product_Service_type.description: The category or type of the product or service.
Product_Service_type.annotation_note: Categorical type label for product classification. Not an IFRS account type.

id.description: Primary key for each vendor record.

businessID.description: Identifier linking the vendor to a business.

Vendor_name.annotation_note: Text identifier. Used for vendor-level filtering.

Billing_address.description: Billing street address of the vendor.

Billing_ZIP_code.description: Billing ZIP code of the vendor.
Billing_ZIP_code.annotation_note: Postal code stored as integer. Not a financial measure.

Balance.description: Outstanding accounts payable balance owed to this vendor.
Balance.annotation_note: AP balance per vendor. Liabilities are credit-normal — they increase on the credit side. Stock measure — represents what is owed at a point in time. A question about outstanding vendor payables should use this column. Contrast with customers.Balance which is an asset (debit-normal AR). Confusing the two is a D1 account_type violation.
